task: I’ve got a couple cheap items I want to list locally, and I need to decide how to set them up so they don’t sit around forever. What’s a simple way to handle the price, photos, and meetup details so the listing gets attention without a lot of back-and-forth?

turns.agent_response:
Use this:

## Price
- set it **just under similar local listings**
- leave a little room for a small offer
- don’t price it so high that people start negotiating hard

## Photos
- **clear first photo**
- show the **whole item**
- show the **model / label**
- show **damage or missing parts**
- use **daylight** if possible

## Meetup details
- **public place**
- **daylight**
- **exact time**
- **exact spot**
- **short window**
- **message when you’re on the way**

## Listing rule
- be **clear**
- be **honest**
- be **brief**

If you want, I can give you a **copy-paste listing template** for cheap local items.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, basically. But make it tighter.

- Price: under the junk around here, but not stupid-low. Leave a little wiggle.
- Photos: first pic clean and bright, whole item. Then close-ups of model, scratches, missing stuff.
- Meetup: public spot, daytime, one exact place. Short window. No back-and-forth all day.

If you’ve got the template, toss it. I want something I can copy fast.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — copy/paste:

**title:**  
`[item] [model] works / no charger / whatever`

**description:**  
`used [item], [model]. works good / untested. [damage or missing parts]. pickup in [area].`

**meetup text:**  
`i can do [time] at [exact spot]. message me when you’re on the way.`

**price rule:**  
`list it a little under similar stuff, leave a small wiggle, don’t go stupid low`

If you want, I can make it even shorter for **phone / console / laptop** listings.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Tight enough.

I’ll use that and just swap the item in. The phone/console/laptop short versions would actually be useful too, but this is fine for now.
